steps.title: Spot the contrast between syllable rate and information rate
steps.content:
The notes show that while the syllable rate (SR) varied — Spanish second‑fastest at 7.7 syllables per second, Vietnamese sixteenth‑fastest at 5.3 — the information rate (IR) was found to be roughly the same across all 17 languages, averaging about 39 bits per second.
This contrast between varying SR and steady IR is the key signal.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A captures this contrast by noting that although syllable rates differed, the information rate was roughly the same for all languages.
The other choices either focus on just one rate, misstate the relationship between the rates, or add details not in the notes.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
